title: reachability.json
description: Output of bin/reachability for one crash. Written next to report.md so --severity-only can reuse it without hitting the network. Versioned via the schema_version field; downstream consumers should reject documents whose schema_version they do not understand.

schema_version.description: Bumped on any breaking change to this document. v2 added vendored_copies / vendored_copy_hits to separate genuine library callers from projects that just re-include the upstream source.

queried_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp at which the backends were queried.

symbols.description: Symbols queried for this report. ASan-backed reports normally query the selected caller-entry symbol only; manual --symbol and stackless reports may query multiple symbols.

ignore.description: Substrings used to drop hits whose repo path or package name contains any of them. Sourced from --ignore CLI args plus reachability_ignore = [...] in target.toml.

match_mode.description: Schema v2+. entrypoint = a genuine caller matched the selected public/caller entry symbol any-frame, since a real dependant reaches the entry point, not the target's internal crash-state chain. all-frames is accepted for older cached documents. any-frame = union/any-match for manual --symbol or narrative-mined stackless reports.

entry_symbol.description: Schema v2+. The public/caller entry-point symbol whose any-frame match defined external-caller popularity under match_mode=entrypoint. Prefer an explicit Entry report field; otherwise use the deepest product frame in the primary sanitizer fault stack. One of symbols; null when no entry point was identified.

language.description: Schema v2+. Code-search language filter applied to both backends, derived from the target tree (lib/languages.py registry) or the --language override. Empty string means the filter was disabled.

status.description: Schema v2+. Present only on the symbol-less / search-skipped documents (no_symbols, local_symbols_only, external_skipped) emitted when no external search ran.

external_callers.description: Cross-backend deduplicated count of (repo, path) caller hits, after the ignore filter.

external_caller_repos.description: Schema v2+. Distinct caller repos among external_caller_hits — the popularity signal for the library_popular surface tier. Counted over repos, not (repo, path) files, so one repo with many matching files is one dependent project.

external_caller_hits.description: The (repo, path) tuples that produced external_callers, in discovery order. Deduplicated across services and across symbols.

matched_symbols.description: Schema v2+. Every probed symbol this file matched (cross-backend). Length may exceed 1 when multiple manual or stackless-report symbols were queried.

vendored_copies.description: Schema v2+. Hits whose path is a vendored copy of the target's own source (e.g. third_party/pcre2/...). Counted separately from external_callers because these projects ship the bug rather than calling into a library that has it.

vendored_copy_hits.description: Schema v2+. The (repo, path) tuples bucketed as vendored copies, parallel to external_caller_hits.

matched_symbols.description: Schema v2+. Every probed symbol this file matched (cross-backend).

services_responding.description: Subset of services whose status came back ok for at least one symbol. If empty, no reachability signal exists and severity scoring should treat reachability_adjust as 0 (not -12).

services.description: Per-backend detail. The status field is the authoritative source for reachability_adjust.

status.description: ok = backend responded with parseable data (count may legitimately be 0). unavailable = transport error, schema mismatch, or non-2xx HTTP — treat as 'no signal' rather than '0 callers'. not_run = the document was emitted without querying this backend (no_symbols / local_symbols_only / external_skipped paths).

vendored.description: Schema v2+. Hits classified as vendored copies of the upstream source.
